text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Patel  
Riverside Medical Practice  
113 Riverside Way, London SW1A 2AA  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Alex Morgan, DOB: 22-07-1982, Hospital No: M904217 NHS No. 7438201659  
Flat 4, 29 Willow Court, London, SE11 5BT 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Epilepsy with prior head injury (historic), mood and sleep disturbance (patient-reported), vitamin D insufficiency (treated), past variability in pharmacy supply with intermittent adherence impacts, current non-smoker, alcohol within recommended limits. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 1500 mg twice daily.  
Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily.  
Cholecalciferol 1000 IU daily.  
Omeprazole 20 mg once daily.  
Sertraline 50 mg once daily.  

I reviewed Alex in clinic today. He attended alone and brought his personal medicines record. He reports that since his community pharmacy switched brands twice earlier this year (March–April), he has been collecting regular supplies from the hospital pharmacy to avoid missed doses; he feels this reduced the previous variability in refills. He keeps a phone alarm system for dosing and has a weekly pill organiser. Energy levels are fair; sleep is fragmented but improving on a regular bedtime routine. He walks independently and performs light exercise three times per week; no falls. Examination today: alert, oriented, normal speech; cranial nerves intact; tone and power normal in all four limbs; gait steady; no focal neurological deficits elicited. BP 128/76 mmHg, pulse 68 bpm regular, weight stable. 

Seizures: Alex has not described any definite events for over a year. He notes occasional brief moments of "head-fog" under stress but without loss of awareness, automatisms, or post-event confusion; these have not progressed. His seizure diary shows no recorded events since June last year. He attributes improved stability to consistent medication supply and routine. In summary, his epilepsy appears quiescent at present with no clinically evident seizures in the past 12+ months. 

Adherence and supply context: Historically, supply interruptions and brand switches contributed to missed doses and uncertainty over dosing times. Since May this year, he reports 100% adherence supported by blister packs from a single dispensary. We discussed continuing this approach to mitigate recurrence risk. ï¿½ 

Investigations: U&E, LFTs, FBC on 10/09/2025 within reference ranges. Vitamin D 34 ng/mL (adequate on current supplementation). Levetiracetam and lamotrigine levels not routinely monitored—no indication to check at present given stability. 

Plan:  
- Continue Levetiracetam 1500 mg twice daily and Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily without change. Ã‚Â·  
- Maintain single-pharmacy supply; avoid unplanned brand switches; 3-month prescription synchronised to reduce pick-up variability.  
- Reinforce adherence tools (alarms, pill organiser).  
- Safety: standard seizure safety advice reiterated; no driving until meets DVLA guidance; patient currently not driving. â€  
- Review in 9–12 months, earlier if any breakthrough events, adverse effects, or supply problems recur.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: no clinically evident seizures in the past 12+ months

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly discusses seizures. It states: "Alex has not described any definite events for over a year" and "His seizure diary shows no recorded events since June last year." The clinic date is 02 October 2025, and the last recorded event was June 2024. That is at least 15-16 months without seizures. The summary also says "no clinically evident seizures in the past 12+ months." This meets the ≥6 months seizure-free criterion. Therefore, the appropriate normalized label is 'seizure free for multiple year' or a specific number of years if exact. Since it exceeds one year but is not a full two years, the safest normalization per the allowed formats is 'seizure free for multiple year' if we must use 'year' and cannot specify fractions. However, the formats allow numeric values; since it is more than one year but less than two and we can't use fractions, using 'multiple year' is appropriate to convey more than one year without specifying an exact integer number of years.
seizure_frequency_number: no clinically evident seizures in the past 12+ months

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for multiple year) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
